results.rendered_html: saṃ-gha m. (fr. sam + √han) ‘close contact or combination’, any collection or assemblage, heap, multitude, quantity, crowd, host, number (generally with gen. pl. or ifc., e.g. muni-s˚, ‘a multitude of sages’,  BhP. ; śatru-s˚, a host of enemies,  Rājat. ),  MBh. ;  Kāv.  &c.

saṃgha (von han mit sam) m.  P. 3,3,86  (vgl. 6,2,144 ).
